Block.Para: Second Session, Fortieth Parliament,
57 Elizabeth II, 2009
SENATE OF CANADA
BILL S-213
An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (carbon offset tax credit)
first reading, January 27, 2009
THE HONOURABLE SENATOR MITCHELL

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY
This enactment amends the Income Tax Act to provide for a new non-refundable tax credit for carbon offset purchases.
2nd Session, 40th Parliament,
57 Elizabeth II, 2009
senate of canada
BILL S-213
An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (carbon offset tax credit)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. (1) The Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after section 118.03:

ExplNote.Para: Income Tax Act
Clause 1: New.

Para.change: 118.04 (1) The following definitions apply in this section.

Block.MarginalNote:
âapproved offset projectâ
Â« projet de compensation approuvÃ© Â»

Para.change: âapproved offset projectâ means an offset project that is approved, or that is of a type approved, by the Minister under subsection (3).

Block.MarginalNote:
âcarbon offset providerâ
Â« fournisseur de titres de compensation de carbone Â»

Para.change: âcarbon offset providerâ means a person or entity designated by the Minister under subsection (3) as a carbon offset provider.

Block.MarginalNote:
âeligible carbon offset purchase expenseâ
Â« dÃ©pense admissible pour lâachat de titres de compensation de carbone Â»

Para.change: âeligible carbon offset purchase expenseâ in respect of an individual means the amount that is paid to a carbon offset provider (other than a carbon offset provider with whom the individual does not deal at armâs length) for the purpose of investing in one or more approved offset projects (other than an approved offset project that is managed, operated or owned by a person with whom the individual does not deal at armâs length), but does not include an amount required to be paid under any enactment of Canada or of a province.

Block.MarginalNote:
âoffset projectâ
Â« projet de compensation Â»

Para.change: âoffset projectâ means a project, program or enterprise that avoids, reduces, sequesters or displaces, or mitigates the effects of, carbon dioxide emissions.
(2) For the purpose of computing the tax payable under this Part by an individual for a taxation year, there may be deducted the amount determined by the formula
Ais the appropriate percentage for the taxation year; and

Bis the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that is an eligible carbon offset purchase expense paid in the taxation year by the individual and that is evidenced by a receipt filed with the Minister.
(3) The Minister may, for the purposes of this section,
(a) approve an offset project or a type of offset project, taking into account the prescribed criteria; and
(b) designate as a carbon offset provider any person or entity that, in the opinion of the Minister, is suitable to be so designated.
(4) The Minister may, in writing, revoke the approval of an offset project or type of offset project or the designation of a carbon offset provider.

Block.Para: (2) Section 118.04 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
2. (1) The description of C in subsection 118.61(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 2: (1) Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 118.61(1):
118.61 (1) In this section, an individualâs unused tuition, textbook and education tax credits at the end of a taxation year is the amount determined by the formula
where

Cis the lesser of the value of B and the amount that would be the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under this section and any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.3 and 118.7);

Block.Para: Cis the lesser of the value of B and the amount that would be the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under this section and any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.3 and 118.7);
(2) Paragraph 118.61(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: (2) Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 118.61(2):
(2) For the purpose of computing an individualâs tax payable under this Part for a taxation year, there may be deducted the lesser of

(b) the amount that would be the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under this section and any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.3 and 118.7).

Block.Para: (b) the amount that would be the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under this section and any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.3 and 118.7).
3. Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the description of C in section 118.8 of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 3: Existing text of relevant portions of section 118.8:

118.8 For the purpose of computing the tax payable under this Part for a taxation year by an individual who, at any time in the year, is a married person or a person who is in a common-law partnership (other than an individual who, by reason of a breakdown of their marriage or common-law partnership, is living separate and apart from the individualâs spouse or common-law partner at the end of the year and for a period of 90 days commencing in the year), there may be deducted an amount determined by the formula
A + B - C
where
Cis the amount, if any, by which

(a) the amount that would be the spouseâs or common-law partnerâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under subsection 118(1) because of paragraph (c) of the description of B in that subsection or under section 118.61 or 118.7)
exceeds
(b) the lesser of

(ii) the amount that would be the spouseâs or common-law partnerâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under section 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.3, 118.61 or 118.7).

Block.Para: (ii) the amount that would be the spouseâs or common-law partnerâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under section 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.3, 118.61 or 118.7).
4. The description of B in paragraph 118.81(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 4: Existing text of relevant portions of section 118.81:
118.81 In this subdivision, the tuition, textbook and education tax credits transferred for a taxation year by a person to an individual is the lesser of
(a) the amount determined by the formula
A - B
where

Bis the amount that would be the personâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.3, 118.61 and 118.7), and

Block.Para: Bis the amount that would be the personâs tax payable under this Part for the year if no amount were deductible under this Division (other than an amount deductible under any of sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.3, 118.61 and 118.7), and
5. Subparagraph 118.91(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 5: Existing text of relevant portions of section 118.91:

118.91 Notwithstanding sections 118 to 118.9, where an individual is resident in Canada throughout part of a taxation year and throughout another part of the year is non-resident, for the purpose of computing the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the year,
(b) the individual shall be allowed only

(i) such of the deductions permitted under subsections 118(3), (10) and 118.6(2.1) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.1, 118.2, 118.5, 118.6, 118.62 and 118.7 as can reasonably be considered wholly applicable, and

Block.Para: (i) such of the deductions permitted under subsections 118(3) and (10) and 118.6(2.1) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.1, 118.2, 118.5, 118.6, 118.62 and 118.7 as can reasonably be considered wholly applicable, and
6. Section 118.92 of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 6: Existing text of section 118.92:

118.92 In computing an individualâs tax payable under this Part, the following provisions shall be applied in the following order: subsections 118(1) and (2), section 118.7, subsections 118(3) and (10) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.3, 118.61, 118.5, 118.6, 118.9, 118.8, 118.2, 118.1, 118.62 and 121.

Block.Para: 118.92 In computing an individualâs tax payable under this Part, the following provisions shall be applied in the following order: subsections 118(1) and (2), section 118.7, subsections 118(3) and (10) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.3, 118.61, 118.5, 118.6, 118.9, 118.8, 118.2, 118.1, 118.62 and 121.
7. Section 118.94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 7: Existing text of section 118.94:

118.94 Sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03 and 118.2, subsections 118.3(2) and (3) and sections 118.6, 118.8 and 118.9 do not apply for the purpose of computing the tax payable under this Part for a taxation year by an individual who at no time in the year is resident in Canada unless all or substantially all of the individualâs income for the year is included in computing the individualâs taxable income earned in Canada for the year.

Block.Para: 118.94 Sections 118, 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04 and 118.2, subsections 118.3(2) and (3) and sections 118.6, 118.8 and 118.9 do not apply for the purpose of computing the tax payable under this Part for a taxation year by an individual who at no time in the year is resident in Canada unless all or substantially all of the individualâs income for the year is included in computing the individualâs taxable income earned in Canada for the year.
8. Paragraph 118.95(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 8: Existing text of relevant portions of section 118.95:

118.95 Notwithstanding sections 118 to 118.9, for the purpose of computing an individualâs tax payable under this Part for a taxation year that ends in a calendar year in which the individual becomes bankrupt, the individual shall be allowed only

(a) such of the deductions as the individual is entitled to under subsections 118(3) and (10) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.1, 118.2, 118.5, 118.6, 118.62 and 118.7 as can reasonably be considered wholly applicable to the taxation year, and

Block.Para: (a) such of the deductions as the individual is entitled to under subsections 118(3) and (10) and sections 118.01, 118.02, 118.03, 118.04, 118.1, 118.2, 118.5, 118.6, 118.62 and 118.7 as can reasonably be considered wholly applicable to the taxation year, and
9. Paragraph 127.531(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 9: Existing text of relevant portions of section 127.531:
127.531 An individualâs basic minimum tax credit for a taxation year is the total of all amounts each of which is

(a) an amount deducted under subsection 118(1), (2) or (10), 118.01(2), 118.02(2), 118.03(2) or 118.3(1) or any of sections 118.5 to 118.7 in computing the individualâs tax payable for the year under this Part; or

Block.Para: (a) an amount deducted under subsection 118(1), (2) or (10), 118.01(2), 118.02(2), 118.03(2), 118.04(2) or 118.3(1) or any of sections 118.5 to 118.7 in computing the individualâs tax payable for the year under this Part; or
10. Clause 128(2)(e)(iii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

ExplNote.Para: Clause 10: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 128(2):
(2) Where an individual has become a bankrupt, the following rules are applicable:

(e) where the individual was a bankrupt at any time in a calendar year the trustee shall, within 90 days from the end of the year, file a return with the Minister, in prescribed form, on behalf of the individual of the individualâs income for any taxation year occurring in the calendar year computed as if
(iii) in computing the individualâs tax payable under this Part for that taxation year, no deduction were allowed

Block.Para: 11. Sections 2 to 10 apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
